toolReviews.title: NotebookLM for Source-Based Study
toolReviews.summary: Students rate it highly for lecture notes, PDFs and source-grounded revision.
toolReviews.description: A student-facing review of where NotebookLM helps, where it needs checking and when a different tool may be better.
toolReviews.author: Review by Amelia R.
limitations: Only as good as the sources you upload
Can miss context when slides are incomplete
Still needs checking before coursework use
toolReviews.studentVerdict: Excellent for source-grounded revision, but it should support your reading rather than replace it.
toolReviews.checkingAdvice: Open the cited source before trusting an answer, especially where the response combines points from several documents.

toolReviews.title: Copilot for Planning and First Draft Questions
toolReviews.summary: Useful for breaking down tasks, but needs clear boundaries around assessment work.
toolReviews.description: A student review of Copilot as a planning companion for study tasks, presentations and reflective prompts.
toolReviews.author: Review by Daniel T.
limitations: Generic advice if the prompt is vague
Needs careful use around assessment rules
toolReviews.studentVerdict: Best when you ask for questions, options and checks rather than finished text.
toolReviews.checkingAdvice: Keep your own notes and final decisions visible so the tool does not blur authorship.
